commit.message:
fix(setup): tailor GitHub token guidance for project ownership (#76)

Summary:
- reorder projects_v2 setup so project URL parsing happens before token entry
- render GitHub token help differently for user-owned, org-owned, and unknown Projects v2
- align setup docs with the new fine-grained-vs-classic PAT guidance

Rationale:
- users were looking for fine-grained Projects permissions before setup had enough context to explain why they were missing
- showing ownership-aware guidance at token entry reduces confusion without adding a new prompt

Tests:
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test setup::tests::
- cargo test (fails in providers::claude::runtime::tests::reads_stdout_while_large_prompt_is_still_being_written)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

projective SL basis rewrite

commit.message:
chore(deps): consolidate Dependabot Go module minor upgrades (#1082)

### Motivation
- Consolidate multiple open Dependabot Go module minor updates into a
single rollup to reduce PR churn and follow the repository's
minor-upgrade guidance (`go get -t -u ./...`).
- Ensure the module `go` directive is updated where required so upgraded
dependencies that require a newer Go toolchain resolve correctly.

### Description
- Updated `go.mod` and `go.sum` to apply consolidated minor-version
upgrades for: `github.com/cli/cli/v2` (→ `v2.87.3`),
`github.com/mark3labs/mcp-go` (→ `v0.45.0`), the
`go.opentelemetry.io/otel` family (→ `v1.42.0`), `golang.org/x/sync` (→
`v0.20.0`), `golang.org/x/sys` (→ `v0.42.0`), `google.golang.org/api` (→
`v0.270.0`), `google.golang.org/grpc` (→ `v1.79.3`), and
`mvdan.cc/sh/v3` (→ `v3.13.0`).
- Updated the module `go` directive from `1.25.2` to `1.25.7` and
refreshed dependency checksums in `go.sum`.
- This PR consolidates the changes from Dependabot PRs: `#1077`,
`#1073`, `#1072`, `#1071`, `#1070`, `#1069`, `#1068`, `#1067`, `#1066`,
`#1065`, and `#1052`.

### Testing
- Ran `go get -t -u ./...` which downloaded and resolved the upgraded
modules and allowed `go` tool to switch to a compatible toolchain
version as required (succeeded for dependency resolution).
- Ran `go mod tidy` to prune and verify module graph (succeeded).
- Attempted `runme run lint test` but the `runme` CLI is not available
in this environment, so ran `make lint` and `make test` instead; `make
lint` failed in this environment because the `checklocks` vet tool is
not present, and `make test` executed but produced multiple failing
tests driven by environment/tooling/network assumptions (examples:
command package tests expecting `deno`/pty behavior, document/editor
tests expecting specific frontmatter/version behavior, and OIDC tests
requiring external network access), while many other packages' tests
passed.

------
[Codex
Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/tasks/task_e_69cab28c63c08324ad7ace6d59e2f0d6)

Signed-off-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Signed-off-by: Sebastian (Tiedtke) Huckleberry <sebastiantiedtke@gmail.com>

commit.message:
release(plugin): prepare 5.3.2 candidate

Summary:
- preserve completed local Pi tool-call state in ChatView so Pi tool
  executions do not get rerouted through the hosted tool loop
- pin Studio Pi auth inventory to the bundled auth-storage path and
  expand macOS and Windows provider-connected coverage on
  openai/gpt-5.4-mini
- harden Windows native release prep with runtime-version refresh,
  redacted bootstrap diagnostics, updated release notes, and 5.3.2
  version metadata

Rationale:
- keep canonical Pi auth and tool-calling paths strict instead of
  masking breakage behind fallbacks
- make the Windows release lane reproducible and future-agent friendly
  so stale runtimes and transient host noise stop causing trial and
  error
- capture the actual 5.3.2 release evidence in-tree before any publish
  step

Tests:
- node --test testing/native/desktop-automation/runner.test.mjs
  testing/native/device/windows/bootstrap.test.mjs
  testing/native/device/windows/runtime-version.test.mjs
  testing/native/device/windows/run-desktop-automation.test.mjs
  testing/native/device/windows/run-clean-install-parity.test.mjs
  testing/native/device/windows/remote-run.test.mjs
- bash autoresearch.sh
- npm test
- npm run build
- npm run check:plugin
- SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_ID=openrouter
  SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_MODEL_ID=openai/gpt-5.4-mini
  SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_API_KEY="$OPENROUTER_API_KEY"
  npm run test:native:desktop:provider-connected
- SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_ID=openrouter
  SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_MODEL_ID=openai/gpt-5.4-mini
  SYSTEMSCULPT_DESKTOP_PROVIDER_API_KEY="$OPENROUTER_API_KEY"
  npm run test:native:windows:provider-connected
- npm run check:release:native
- git diff --check
- gitleaks git . --log-opts='5.3.1..HEAD'

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
chore(parquet): upgrade arrow and parquet to 58.1.0 (#64)

## Why
- keep the Arrow and Parquet stack current to pick up upstream fixes and
improvements
- clear the outdated dependency status reported for `arrow` and
`parquet`

## What
- upgrade `arrow` from `57.0.0` to `58.1.0`
- upgrade `parquet` from `57.0.0` to `58.1.0` and refresh the lockfile
- switch to `set_max_row_group_row_count` to match the current Parquet
writer API

## Testing
- `cargo test`
- `just before_commit`

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Keep deploy docs anchored to the canonical guides

The README had grown into a second copy of the install and target-host
operator guides, which made the docs harder to keep aligned and blurred
the boundary between orchestration surfaces and the mutable ranctl path.
This change deletes repeated prose from the README, fixes one broken
examples link, and aligns the same boundary language across the docs
hub, the Symphony/Codex/skills architecture note, and the install /
real-server guides.

Constraint: Keep operator commands and proof boundaries unchanged
Rejected: Rewrite the guides into a new docs structure | unnecessary scope expansion for a deslop lane
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep detailed target-host dashboard exposure guidance canonical in 18 and prefer README pointers over duplicate walkthrough text
Tested: npm run docs:build; mise exec -- mix contract_ci; manual cross-link and proof-boundary review across README.md, docs/index.md, and architecture docs 06/12/16/17/18
Not-tested: Remote operator walkthrough execution on a live host
Related: YON-115
Related: YON-112

Co-authored-by: mud-the-developer <mud-the-developer@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Deslop ran_action_gateway and ran_config request/policy glue

Collapse the duplicated backend parsing in `RanActionGateway.Request`
and move policy description formatting into `RanConfig.ChangePolicy`
so the runner and config layer share one request/policy boundary.
The cleanup stays inside the selected file cluster and adds focused
regression tests for backend normalization and omitted-target policy
handling before reusing the formatter from the runner.

Constraint: Standard backends must still normalize to atoms while replacement backends stay as request strings
Rejected: Introduce a new shared glue module | adds another boundary for a small cleanup pass
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Keep request normalization and policy description changes paired with the focused request and policy tests
Tested: mix test apps/ran_action_gateway/test/ran_action_gateway/request_test.exs apps/ran_config/test/ran_config/change_policy_test.exs apps/ran_action_gateway/test/ran_action_gateway/cli_test.exs:220 apps/ran_action_gateway/test/ran_action_gateway/cli_test.exs:1112 apps/ran_action_gateway/test/ran_action_gateway/cli_test.exs:1134
Tested: mix compile --warnings-as-errors
Tested: mix contract_ci

commit.message:
Filter non-repo Docker containers out of Mission Control

The runtime board was still trusting broad `ran-*` name and compose
project prefixes after the first filtering pass. That let unrelated
stacks from other repositories survive whenever their container names
looked similar to the local OAI or agent lanes.

This change makes the compose working directory the primary ownership
signal by resolving it against the current repository's git common-dir.
Known `ran-oai` and `ran-agent-local` prefixes remain only as a narrow
fallback when Docker does not expose the workdir label. The snapshot
fixture now proves both sides of the contract: repo-local OAI and xApp
lanes stay visible, while foreign `ran-grafana` and `ran-agent-local`
compose workloads are dropped.

Constraint: Runtime board input is limited to `docker ps -a` metadata
Rejected: Keep broad `ran-*` matching | still leaks foreign compose
 stacks from other repos
Confidence: high
Scope-risk: narrow
Reversibility: clean
Directive: Treat compose working_dir as the primary ownership signal;
 only broaden prefix fallback after verifying a repo-owned lane lacks
 that label in production
Tested: `mix test apps/ran_observability/test/ran_observability/
 dashboard_snapshot_test.exs`
Tested: `mix run -e '...YON111ReproRunner...'`
Tested: `bin/ran-dashboard`; `curl http://127.0.0.1:4050/api/health`;
 `curl http://127.0.0.1:4050/api/dashboard`
Not-tested: Playwright PNG capture; local Chrome install required sudo

Co-authored-by: mud-the-developer <mud-the-developer@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(jumia): add max_age=0 to force fresh OAuth session on retry (#990)

* fix(tests): handle prebuild-generated gradle.properties in SDK 55 test

expo prebuild adds newArchEnabled to android/gradle.properties, but
this file is gitignored. Skip the check when the file doesn't exist
and filter out prebuild-injected lines when it does.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(mobile-storefront): cast ctaLink to satisfy Expo Router typed routes

The HeroSlide ctaLink type expects Expo Router's route union, but
the slides come from dynamic config data as plain strings. Cast
to the expected type since the links are validated at runtime.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): add max_age=0 to force fresh OAuth session on retry

Jumia's /login endpoint detects prior sessions and redirects to
/connect/auth/callback with a stale session id, returning "no route
matched". Adding max_age=0 (OpenID Connect) forces Jumia to ignore
cached sessions and always present a fresh login page.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

to the latest `dev` branch revision `d5c2058`

commit.message:
Handle bad C6 radical splits gracefully (#419)

Treat inconsistent C6 radical block data as failure, instead of
raising errors: it simply can happen that the initial
determination that we are in C6 case turns out to be wrong, and we
have to abort.

Perhaps there is a deeper issue here with the math in that case,
but for now this seems plausible and fixes a concrete issue.

Fixes #295

commit.message:
fix(mobile-admin): use system default notification sound (#986)

* fix(mobile-admin): use system default notification sound

sound: 'default' tells expo-notifications to look for a custom sound
file named "default" in res/raw/, which doesn't exist. Use null to
play the system default notification sound instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(mobile-admin): remove sound: null to use system default sounds

Removing the sound property entirely lets Android use the system
default sound based on channel importance (HIGH channels get sound,
LOW channels stay silent). sound: null explicitly silences channels.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Mark PostgreSQL package as side-effectful

The package applies Temporal polyfills at module load time, so its
package metadata must not advertise the module as side-effect free.

Set sideEffects to true so bundlers preserve the initialization when
consumers import the package.

https://github.com/fedify-dev/botkit/pull/19#discussion_r3008937579

commit.message:
Split release workflow tools from tag source

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: resolve guest metadata from project dir (#39)

## Summary
- run `cargo metadata` from the resolved guest project directory instead
of the invocation cwd
- keep `--dist` and other invocation-cwd-relative semantics unchanged by
avoiding a global process `chdir`
- add a regression test that records the cwd used by the metadata
subprocess

## Problem
`cargo airbender build --project <path>` already runs `cargo build` and
`cargo objcopy` from the guest directory, but manifest discovery still
called `cargo metadata` from the caller's cwd. With rustup, that means
`rust-toolchain.toml` resolution followed the invocation directory
instead of the selected guest project.

## Why this approach
Switching the whole process cwd would also rebase `--dist` and other
caller-relative paths. The smaller fix is to align just the metadata
subprocess with the existing build/objcopy behavior.

## Testing
- `cargo nextest run -p airbender-build --lib`
- `cargo test -p cargo-airbender --bin cargo-airbender
--no-default-features commands::build::tests -- --nocapture`
- `cargo build -p cargo-airbender --no-default-features`
- Manual toolchain-resolution repro with `rustup override set 1.88.0`
outside the project and `rust-toolchain.toml` pinned to `1.91.0` inside
the guest: confirmed plain `cargo metadata --manifest-path ...` follows
the caller cwd/toolchain unless `current_dir` is set
- Manual end-to-end `target/debug/cargo-airbender build --project ...`
with a wrapper `cargo` script: confirmed `metadata`, `build`, and all
`objcopy` calls now run from the guest directory and pick up the
workspace `rust-toolchain.toml`; also confirmed `--dist` output still
lands under the invocation-cwd-relative output path

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(web): migrate raw timingSafeEqual to HMAC-based constantTimeEqual (#982)

* fix(web): migrate raw timingSafeEqual to HMAC-based constantTimeEqual

Replaces raw `timingSafeEqual` with our shared `constantTimeEqual()` utility
across 11 API routes + 1 auth helper. The HMAC approach prevents length-leak
timing attacks by producing fixed-length digests regardless of input.

Also restores the `constant-time-equal.ts` utility that was missing from main,
fixes the agentic auth (was using plain `===`), and adds colocated tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(web): address CodeRabbit review on constant-time-equal migration

- Add Unicode and null-byte edge-case tests for constantTimeEqual

  constructing the expected token string

commit.message:
Self-contain Docker workflow and seed state (#74)

* feat(docker): self-contain workflow and seed state

Summary:
- move Docker workflow and seed repo state into named volumes
- load repo-root WORKFLOW.md prompt and hooks for Docker workspaces
- add Docker setup, seed sync, and config import/export helpers

Rationale:
- remove steady-state host bind dependencies from Docker mode
- let workspace behavior come from the configured repo with a safe fallback
- keep deployment config separate from repo-local prompt and hook behavior

Tests:
- cargo fmt --check --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml
- cargo clippy --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings
- docker compose -p kairastra-test -f rust/compose.yml --env-file rust/.env.example config
- bash -n scripts/install-remote-docker.sh
- make -C rust -n docker-setup
- make -C rust -n docker-doctor

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* docs(agents): fix gh template invocation

* docs: clarify docker workflow model

* docs(agents): clarify gh as GitHub CLI

commit.message:
refactor(tests): remove dead_code allowances (#10)

## Why
- Reduce `dead_code` suppression noise in the test tree.
- Keep each integration test target compiling only the helpers it
actually uses.

## What
- Split the shared `tests/support/mod.rs` helper into smaller support
modules grouped by use case.
- Update each integration test file to import only the support it needs.
- Replace the test-only `MeasureOptions::with_guess` helper with direct
`MeasureOptions` struct construction.

## Testing
- `cargo check --tests`
- `cargo test anafanafo::tests::anafanafo_matches_reference_cases`
- `cargo test badge_maker_matches_reference_cases`
- `cargo test raw_css_variable_input_falls_back_like_badge_maker`
- `cargo test invalid_id_suffix_matches_badge_maker_error`

## Risks
- This removes the public `MeasureOptions::with_guess` helper. External
callers should construct `MeasureOptions { font, guess }` directly
instead.

---------

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>
